title: bee-scribing bootstrap mode: skeleton generator for system-overview and reading-map
action: Add a fourth mode 'bootstrap' to bee-scribing (per D2). SKILL.md modes table gains the row: trigger = docs/specs/ missing system-overview.md or reading-map.md (typically right after onboarding); does = offer (never auto-run) a bounded skeleton pass creating ONLY the missing file(s) with mechanically provable facts — an existing map file is never touched by bootstrap (in-place-never-fork holds). In the reference, add a Bootstrap section with the binding rules: sources are code/tree inspection and README verbatim extracts only; every meaning, purpose, or rule that code cannot prove is an Open Gap, never invented; frontmatter coverage: partial; NO user interviews (meaning-filling belongs to harvest mode — bootstrap is inventory, harvest is meaning); skeleton shapes for both files (system-overview: area-map stub from top-level structure + entry points + README first paragraph as quoted provenance; reading-map: one line per top-level location with a mechanically-derived one-liner or an [unknown] gap marker); bootstrap output must state its own gaps loudly so the Fresh Session Test probe (grooming) and harvest have a worklist. Tech-agnostic collision rule (binding, in the reference section): directory paths live only in reading-map lines and Pointers; a system-overview area-map line that cannot be stated in business terms carries an [unknown] gap marker instead; a README quote naming technology goes to Pointers or becomes a gap — never into the Purpose paragraph. Insert the Bootstrap section immediately after the Harvest Interview Protocol section of the reference. In bee-hive SKILL.md Session Scout section, add one sentence after the state-layer paragraph: when docs/specs/ lacks the two map files, offer bee-scribing bootstrap (user-approved, never silent). Respect the <200-line SKILL.md budget: the SKILL.md additions stay within a table row + a mode sentence; details live in the reference.

truths: bee-scribing SKILL.md modes table lists bootstrap with its trigger and offer-never-auto-run semantics
The reference's Bootstrap section forbids interviews and invented meanings, requires coverage: partial and loud Open Gaps, and carries skeleton shapes for both files
bee-hive Session Scout offers bootstrap when docs/specs/ lacks system-overview.md or reading-map.md
Bootstrap creates only missing map files — an existing system-overview.md or reading-map.md is never overwritten

artifacts.substantive: Bootstrap section with binding rules + both skeleton shapes, no TODO stubs

key_links: SKILL.md modes table row points to the reference section for the full rules
prohibitions: No mechanical/code changes in this cell (prose only)
No auto-run semantics anywhere — bootstrap is always offered and user-approved (per D2)
SKILL.md files stay under 200 lines each

verify: grep -q "bootstrap" skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md && grep -q "Bootstrap" skills/bee-scribing/references/scribing-reference.md && grep -qi "bootstrap" skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && [ $(wc -l < skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md) -le 200 ] && [ $(wc -l < skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md) -le 200 ] && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs

trace.outcome: bee-scribing gained a fourth mode 'bootstrap' (D2): modes-table row + inventory-vs-meaning sentence in SKILL.md; full binding rules (sources = tree + verbatim README only, never-invent, coverage: partial, no interviews, loud gaps), tech-agnostic collision rule, and skeleton shapes for system-overview.md and reading-map.md in a new Bootstrap section of scribing-reference.md placed after the Harvest Interview Protocol; bee-hive Session Scout now offers bootstrap when docs/specs/ lacks either map file (user-approved, never auto-run). Prose only; both SKILL.md files well under 200 lines (137 / 151).
trace.verify_output: All greps and line-count guards passed (scribing SKILL.md 137 lines, hive SKILL.md 151 lines, both <=200). test_lib.mjs: 47 passed, 0 failed. test_onboard_bee.mjs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 0. Exit code 0.
trace.verify_command: grep -q "bootstrap" skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md && grep -q "Bootstrap" skills/bee-scribing/references/scribing-reference.md && grep -qi "bootstrap" skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md && [ $(wc -l < skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md) -le 200 ] && [ $(wc -l < skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md) -le 200 ] && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs
